text:
Once upon a time, there was a tiny cat with a magnificent mane of black velvet fur. Her name was Luna. Luna loved to explore ancient woodland treacherous with fallen logs and thorny vines.

One moonlit night, the forest was unusually quiet. It was a secret garden where owls chirped melodies at the moon. Luna, having sensed something curious, ventured along the moss-covered path.

There, in a hollow beneath a venerable oak, sat a ladybird eating a fly. Luna sat quietly beside her, staring. The Ladybird fluttered closer, its wing-spots reflecting the moonlight. It spoke up.

"Wouldn't you like to hear a tale about your coat?" asked the ladybird. With that, she began weaving a story of how velvet became so black.

Luna listened enraptured, entranced by the ladybird's tales. The ladybird went on about a hawk that once tried to snatch its fur. They flew away over the hills,
